message.content: Dimension: OBSERVABILITY / PROVENANCE. Probe /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay for span/run lifecycle on error paths, MLflow run termination, provenance completeness/persistence/keying, metrics. If nothing, state what you checked. Return JSON findings: id (P7-<dim>-N), severity, file, line, title, description, poc, suggested_fix. ONLY genuine reproducible defects — do not manufacture nits.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Self-hosted tracing — OpenTelemetry → a local collector (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's Phoenix/OTel bootstrap. Everything is
4	on-box: spans export over OTLP to a loopback-bound collector; nothing leaves the machine.
5	OpenTelemetry and the Phoenix collector are optional (the ``observability`` extra) and
6	imported lazily, so this module loads — and the engine runs untraced — with neither present.
7	
8	The reasoning seam already emits spans through the global OTel tracer provider; calling
9	:func:`bootstrap_tracing` once at startup wires that global provider at a local collector so
10	those spans land somewhere. Without the extra (or with ``ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING`` set),
11	tracing degrades to a silent no-op.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import atexit
17	import logging
18	import os
19	import signal
20	import threading
21	from contextlib import contextmanager
22	from typing import Any, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol
23	
24	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
25	
26	_log = logging.getLogger("assay_engine.observability")
27	
28	# Local collector endpoint (loopback-enforced).
29	TRACING_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_HOST", "localhost")
30	
31	
32	def _int_env(name: str, default: str) -> int:
33	    """Parse an int env var with a clear error naming the var (#CV-S-1, same class as #H-022).
34	
35	    A bare ``int(os.environ[...])`` at import time raises an opaque "invalid literal for int()"
36	    that names neither the var nor fires inside any handler.
37	    """
38	    raw = os.environ.get(name, default)
39	    try:
40	        return int(raw)
41	    except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
42	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be an integer; got {raw!r}") from exc
43	
44	
45	TRACING_PORT = _int_env("ASSAY_TRACING_PORT", "6006")
46	PROJECT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PROJECT", "assay")
47	
48	_provider: Any = None
49	# Guards the _provider check-then-set so concurrent bootstrap_tracing() calls don't race.
50	_provider_lock = threading.Lock()
51	
52	
53	def _disabled() -> bool:
54	    return os.environ.get("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
55	
56	
57	def tracing_base_url() -> str:
58	    require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
59	    return f"http://{TRACING_HOST}:{TRACING_PORT}"
60	
61	
62	def tracing_endpoint() -> str:
63	    """OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint at the local collector (loopback-enforced)."""
64	    return f"{tracing_base_url()}/v1/traces"
65	
66	
67	def bootstrap_tracing() -> Any:
68	    """Idempotently wire the global OTel tracer provider to the local collector.
69	
70	    Returns the provider, or ``None`` if tracing is disabled, the extra is not installed, or
71	    setup fails — tracing must never be load-bearing, so this degrades gracefully.
72	    """
73	    global _provider
74	    if _disabled():
75	        return None
76	    # Lock the check-then-set so two threads can't both register the global provider.
77	    with _provider_lock:
78	        if _provider is not None:
79	            return _provider
80	        try:
81	            require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
82	            # Bound the OTLP export so a downed collector can never hang teardown (#O3).
83	            os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
84	            from phoenix.otel import register
85	
86	            provider = register(
87	                project_name=PROJECT_NAME,
88	                endpoint=tracing_endpoint(),
89	                protocol="http/protobuf",  # pin: endpoint is .../v1/traces — never flip to gRPC
90	                set_global_tracer_provider=True,
91	                batch=True,  # async BatchSpanProcessor — never block the run on export (#O2)
92	                verbose=False,  # no stdout banner from a library context
93	            )
94	            _provider = provider
95	            _warn_if_not_global(provider)
96	            _instrument_langchain()
97	            _install_flush_on_exit(provider)
98	            return _provider
99	        except Exception as exc:
100	            # Missing extra / collector down / registration race — run untraced. Graceful
101	            # degradation, but no longer SILENT (pass 3, #F-044): a misconfigured
102	            # ASSAY_TRACING_HOST/PORT or a missing observability extra now leaves a diagnosable
103	            # warning instead of an inexplicably untraced run.
104	            _log.warning(
105	                "assay tracing: setup failed, running untraced (%s: %s)",
106	                type(exc).__name__,
107	                exc,
108	            )
109	            return None
110	
111	
112	def _warn_if_not_global(provider: Any) -> None:
113	    """If another provider already won the global slot, our manual spans would vanish (#O6)."""
114	    try:
115	        from opentelemetry import trace
116	
117	        if trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
118	            _log.warning(
119	                "assay tracing: another OpenTelemetry provider holds the global slot; "
120	                "manually-emitted spans may not reach the local collector"
121	            )
122	    except Exception:
123	        pass
124	
125	
126	def _instrument_langchain() -> None:
127	    """Capture LangChain auto-spans (the bulk reasoning tier uses LangChain) — best-effort (#O5)."""
128	    try:
129	        from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
130	
131	        LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(skip_dep_check=True)
132	    except Exception:
133	        pass
134	
135	
136	def _install_flush_on_exit(provider: Any) -> None:
137	    """Flush buffered spans on clean exit (atexit) and on SIGTERM (#O3).
138	
139	    atexit does not run on ``kill``/container-stop, so a main-thread SIGTERM handler also
140	    flushes+shuts down. Both are guarded — tracing teardown must never raise.
141	    """
142	    flush = getattr(provider, "force_flush", None)
143	    if not callable(flush):
144	        return
145	    atexit.register(lambda: _safe(flush))
146	    if threading.current_thread() is threading.main_thread():
147	        try:
148	            prior = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)
149	
150	            def _handler(signum: int, frame: Any) -> None:
151	                _safe(flush)
152	                _safe(getattr(provider, "shutdown", lambda: None))
153	                if callable(prior):
154	                    prior(signum, frame)
155	
156	            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
157	        except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - not on main thread / unsupported
158	            pass
159	
160	
161	def _safe(fn: Any) -> None:
162	    try:
163	        fn()
164	    except Exception:
165	        pass
166	
167	
168	@contextmanager
169	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None) -> Iterator[None]:
170	    """Stamp ``run_id`` onto every span emitted inside the block (cross-store correlation).
171	
172	    A no-op when ``run_id`` is None or OpenTelemetry is absent.
173	    """
174	    if run_id is None:
175	        yield
176	        return
177	    try:
178	        from opentelemetry import baggage, context
179	    except ImportError:
180	        yield
181	        return
182	    token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("assay.run_id", run_id))
183	    try:
184	        yield
185	    finally:
186	        context.detach(token)
187	
188	
189	# OpenInference requires every span carry a span kind so Phoenix classifies it (CHAIN/LLM/
190	# AGENT/TOOL); a plain OTel span with no kind renders as UNKNOWN in the Phoenix UI. We set the
191	# raw semantic-convention attribute directly (no openinference import needed for a manual span).
192	_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND = "openinference.span.kind"
193	
194	
195	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
196	class Tracer(Protocol):
197	    @contextmanager
198	    def span(
199	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
200	    ) -> Iterator[None]: ...
201	
202	
203	class OtelTracer:
204	    """A tracer that emits OpenTelemetry spans via the global provider (no-op if OTel absent).
205	
206	    Use this where a component wants explicit spans rather than relying on auto-instrumentation;
207	    it honors whatever provider :func:`bootstrap_tracing` registered. Each span is stamped with
208	    its OpenInference ``kind`` so Phoenix classifies it (default ``CHAIN``).
209	    """
210	
211	    @contextmanager
212	    def span(
213	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
214	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
215	        try:
216	            from opentelemetry import trace
217	        except ImportError:
218	            yield
219	            return
220	        tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine")
221	        # start_as_current_span defaults record_exception=True and set_status_on_exception=True,
222	        # so an exception escaping the body is automatically recorded as a span event and the span
223	        # status is set to ERROR with the exception text (verified in test_observability) — #F-005
224	        # was a false positive; no manual error-recording is needed here.
225	        with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as sp:
226	            sp.set_attribute(_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND, kind)
227	            for k, v in (attributes or {}).items():
228	                sp.set_attribute(k, v)
229	            yield
230	
231	
232	class UnconfiguredTracer:
233	    """Fail-loud placeholder for contexts that must not silently drop spans."""
234	
235	    @contextmanager
236	    def span(
237	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
238	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
239	        raise NotImplementedError(
240	            "no tracer configured — call bootstrap_tracing() and use OtelTracer, or supply a "
241	            "test double"
242	        )
243	        yield  # pragma: no cover - keeps this a generator/contextmanager
244

1	"""Experiment tracking — a local, on-box run/metric/artifact store (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's MLflow setup. The backend is a local store
4	(default: a SQLite file under the working directory); a remote tracking server is rejected by
5	the local-URI guard. MLflow is optional (the ``observability`` extra) and imported lazily, so
6	this module loads without it.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
16	
17	_DEFAULT_URI = "sqlite:///assay_mlflow.db"
18	EXPERIMENT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_EXPERIMENT", "assay")
19	
20	
21	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
22	    """Resolve the tracking URI (env override, else local SQLite), enforced local."""
23	    uri = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", _DEFAULT_URI)
24	    # Resolve a relative sqlite path to an absolute file so runs land deterministically.
25	    prefix = "sqlite:///"
26	    if uri.startswith(prefix):
27	        rel = uri[len(prefix) :]
28	        if not os.path.isabs(rel):
29	            uri = prefix + str((Path.cwd() / rel).resolve())
30	    return require_local_uri(uri, what="experiment tracking")
31	
32	
33	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
34	class ExperimentTracker(Protocol):
35	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str: ...
36	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None: ...
37	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None: ...
38	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None: ...
39	
40	
41	class MlflowExperimentTracker:
42	    """A local-store experiment tracker backed by MLflow (lazy import).
43	
44	    Construction validates the tracking URI is local and selects the experiment; it does not
45	    import MLflow until a run is started, so the module is importable without the extra.
46	    """
47	
48	    def __init__(self, experiment: str = EXPERIMENT_NAME) -> None:
49	        self._experiment = experiment
50	        self._uri = get_tracking_uri()  # validates local before anything else
51	
52	    def _client(self) -> Any:
53	        """An MlflowClient bound to the local store. Uses the client API (keyed by run_id)
54	        throughout, so no run ever sits on the global active-run stack — start_run followed by
55	        log_metric cannot collide (audit issue #O1)."""
56	        try:
57	            from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
58	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
59	            raise RuntimeError(
60	                "experiment tracking requires the 'observability' extra (mlflow) — not installed"
61	            ) from exc
62	        return MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self._uri)
63	
64	    def _experiment_id(self, client: Any) -> str:
65	        exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
66	        if exp is not None:
67	            return str(exp.experiment_id)
68	        return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
69	
70	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
71	        client = self._client()
72	        run = client.create_run(self._experiment_id(client), run_name=name)
73	        run_id = str(run.info.run_id)
74	        # The run now exists in the store. If param-logging fails partway (network blip, server
75	        # error), the run would otherwise be orphaned permanently in RUNNING state — phantom runs
76	        # accumulate over time (pass 3, #F-023). Terminate it FAILED on any param-logging error
77	        # before re-raising, so a created run is never left dangling.
78	        try:
79	            for k, v in dict(params).items():
80	                client.log_param(run_id, k, v)
81	        except Exception:
82	            try:
83	                client.set_terminated(run_id, status="FAILED")
84	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 - best-effort cleanup; original error re-raised
85	                pass
86	            raise
87	        return run_id
88	
89	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None:
90	        self._client().log_metric(run_id, key, value)
91	
92	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None:
93	        self._client().log_artifact(run_id, path)
94	
95	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None:
96	        # MLflow run statuses: FINISHED / FAILED / KILLED. Recording FAILED makes a failed run
97	        # distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110).
98	        self._client().set_terminated(run_id, status=status)
99

1	"""Append-only provenance trail (GOVERNANCE §3, W3C PROV-DM style).
2	
3	Every action a study takes is recorded, in order, before the next executes: ingestion, the
4	blind baseline build, each discovered/locked hypothesis, every gate decision, each verdict,
5	the source scorecard, the final report. The trail is the spine of reproducibility and of the
6	independence claim — "no step can be retroactively removed or reordered" (GOVERNANCE §3).
7	
8	The trail enforces that structurally, not by promise:
9	
10	- **Append-only.** Entries are added through :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.record` only; there is no
11	  remove/edit/reorder API and the exposed view is an immutable tuple of frozen entries.
12	- **Chained.** Each entry carries ``prev_hash`` and an ``entry_hash`` over
13	  ``(prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp)`` using the engine's one type-faithful
14	  serializer (:mod:`assay_engine._canonical`), rooted at a fixed genesis.
15	
16	Integrity — honest scope, two tiers:
17	
18	- **Unkeyed (default).** The chain is plain SHA-256. It is *tamper-evident against naive
19	  tampering and accidental corruption*: editing one entry without recomputing downstream
20	  hashes, reordering, or deleting an entry is caught by :func:`verify_records`. It is **not**
21	  forgery-proof: a motivated party who controls the serialized bytes can edit a payload and
22	  recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain, and the result verifies. Unkeyed integrity buys
23	  "is this a valid chain?", not "is this *the* chain the engine produced."
24	- **Keyed (``secret=...``).** Pass an on-box secret (as pre-registration does, ADR-0009) and
25	  the chain is HMAC-SHA256. The head cannot be recomputed without the secret, so a downstream
26	  store cannot silently rewrite history and re-seal it; :func:`verify_records` must be given
27	  the same secret. This is local tamper-*resistance*; non-repudiable third-party attestation of
28	  the trail's time remains the same pluggable concern as pre-registration, out of scope here.
29	"""
30	
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import datetime as _dt
34	import hashlib
35	import hmac
36	import threading
37	from dataclasses import dataclass
38	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping, cast
39	
40	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_json
41	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping, unfreeze
42	
43	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
44	_GENESIS = "assay-provenance:v1:genesis"
45	
46	Clock = Callable[[], _dt.datetime]
47	
48	
49	class ProvenanceError(RuntimeError):
50	    """The provenance trail is inconsistent (a broken chain) or an entry cannot be recorded."""
51	
52	
53	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
54	class ProvenanceEntry:
55	    """One immutable, chained record of a single action."""
56	
57	    seq: int
58	    kind: str
59	    summary: str
60	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
61	    timestamp: str  # ISO-8601 UTC
62	    prev_hash: str
63	    entry_hash: str
64	
65	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
66	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
67	
68	
69	def _digest(
70	    seq: int,
71	    kind: str,
72	    summary: str,
73	    payload: Mapping[str, Any],
74	    timestamp: str,
75	    prev_hash: str,
76	    secret: bytes | None,
77	) -> str:
78	    try:
79	        msg = canonical_json(
80	            ["assay-prov:v1", prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, dict(payload), timestamp]
81	        ).encode("utf-8")
82	    except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
83	        # RecursionError (deeply nested payload) is a RuntimeError, not Value/TypeError — catch it
84	        # too so the entrypoints surface a typed ProvenanceError, never a raw exception (#89).
85	        raise ProvenanceError(
86	            f"provenance payload for {kind!r} cannot be serialized reproducibly ({exc}); "
87	            "record only JSON-native / hashable values of bounded depth"
88	        ) from None
89	    if secret is None:
90	        return hashlib.sha256(msg).hexdigest()
91	    return hmac.new(secret, msg, "sha256").hexdigest()
92	
93	
94	def _utc_now() -> _dt.datetime:
95	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
96	
97	
98	class ProvenanceTrail:
99	    """An append-only, chained audit trail for one study run.
100	
101	    ``secret`` (>= 16 bytes) keys the chain with HMAC so a serialized trail cannot be forged
102	    without it (see module docstring). ``clock`` injects the timestamp source (deterministic
103	    tests); it must return a timezone-aware ``datetime``.
104	    """
105	
106	    def __init__(self, *, secret: bytes | None = None, clock: Clock | None = None) -> None:
107	        if secret is not None and (not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16):
108	            raise ValueError("provenance secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
109	        self._entries: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
110	        self._secret: bytes | None = bytes(secret) if secret is not None else None
111	        self._clock: Clock = clock or _utc_now
112	        # serialize the read-compute-append so concurrent record() calls cannot interleave and
113	        # corrupt the seq/prev_hash chain (#114)
114	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
115	
116	    def record(self, kind: str, summary: str, **payload: Any) -> ProvenanceEntry:
117	        """Append one entry. Returns it. The only way to add to the trail. Thread-safe."""
118	        if not kind:
119	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance entry kind must be non-empty")
120	        with self._lock:
121	            return self._record_locked(kind, summary, payload)
122	
123	    def _record_locked(
124	        self, kind: str, summary: str, payload: Mapping[str, Any]
125	    ) -> ProvenanceEntry:
126	        seq = len(self._entries)
127	        prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
128	        try:
129	            instant = self._clock()
130	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the clock is an injected dependency
131	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance clock raised: {exc}") from None
132	        if not isinstance(instant, _dt.datetime):
133	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a datetime")
134	        try:
135	            # utcoffset()/astimezone()/isoformat() can raise on a hostile datetime subclass or an
136	            # out-of-range value — keep the typed-error contract (#96).
137	            if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
138	                raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a timezone-aware datetime")
139	            timestamp = instant.astimezone(_UTC).isoformat()
140	        except ProvenanceError:
141	            raise
142	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
143	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance timestamp could not be normalized: {exc}") from None
144	        entry_hash = _digest(seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp, prev_hash, self._secret)
145	        try:
146	            entry = ProvenanceEntry(
147	                seq=seq,
148	                kind=kind,
149	                summary=summary,
150	                payload=payload,
151	                timestamp=timestamp,
152	                prev_hash=prev_hash,
153	                entry_hash=entry_hash,
154	            )
155	        except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
156	            raise ProvenanceError(
157	                f"provenance entry for {kind!r} could not be frozen ({exc})"
158	            ) from None
159	        self._entries.append(entry)
160	        return entry
161	
162	    def as_recorder(self) -> Callable[[Any], None]:
163	        """A :data:`gates.ProvenanceRecorder` bound to this trail (records GateDecisions).
164	
165	        Reads each attribute exactly once and defensively, so a booby-trapped decision object
166	        (a property that raises) surfaces a typed ``ProvenanceError`` rather than a raw error.
167	        """
168	
169	        def _record(decision: Any) -> None:
170	            try:
171	                gate = getattr(decision, "gate", None)
172	                approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
173	                reason = getattr(decision, "reason", "")
174	                evidence = dict(getattr(decision, "evidence", {}) or {})
175	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the decision is on the trust boundary
176	                raise ProvenanceError(
177	                    f"could not read gate decision for provenance: {exc}"
178	                ) from None
179	            self.record(
180	                "gate",
181	                f"gate {gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
182	                gate=gate,
183	                approved=approved,
184	                reason=reason,
185	                evidence=evidence,
186	            )
187	
188	        return _record
189	
190	    @property
191	    def entries(self) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
192	        return tuple(self._entries)
193	
194	    @property
195	    def head(self) -> str:
196	        """The current chain head (last entry_hash, or the genesis if empty)."""
197	        return self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
198	
199	    def __len__(self) -> int:
200	        return len(self._entries)
201	
202	    def verify(self) -> None:
203	        """Recompute the chain; raise :class:`ProvenanceError` on any inconsistency."""
204	        verify_records(self._entries, secret=self._secret)
205	
206	    def to_records(self) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
207	        """Serialize to plain dicts (for a persistent store); re-checkable via :func:`verify_records`."""
208	        return entries_to_records(self._entries)
209	
210	
211	def entries_to_records(
212	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
213	) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
214	    """Serialize loose entries (e.g. ``StudyResult.provenance``) to plain dicts (#F-045).
215	
216	    The same shape :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` produces, so a caller holding only the
217	    returned entries — not the trail object — can still persist a re-checkable trail.
218	
219	    Keying is NOT embedded (pass 5, #H-017): a keyed (HMAC) trail and an unkeyed (SHA-256) trail
220	    serialize identically — the records carry no key material and no trusted algorithm marker (one
221	    would be spoofable since it is not itself part of the per-entry digest). The verifier must
222	    therefore know out-of-band whether the trail was keyed and supply the same ``secret`` to
223	    :func:`from_records` / :func:`verify_records`; that is deliberate key management, not a gap —
224	    ``verify_records`` fails closed under the wrong/absent secret.
225	    """
226	    return tuple(
227	        {
228	            "seq": e.seq,
229	            "kind": e.kind,
230	            "summary": e.summary,
231	            # deep-thaw nested FrozenDict/tuple values so the record is plain + JSON-serializable
232	            # (#F-045): dict(e.payload) only shallow-converts and leaves nested FrozenDicts (e.g.
233	            # the baseline 'determinism' map) which json.dumps cannot encode.
234	            "payload": cast("dict[str, Any]", unfreeze(e.payload)),
235	            "timestamp": e.timestamp,
236	            "prev_hash": e.prev_hash,
237	            "entry_hash": e.entry_hash,
238	        }
239	        for e in entries
240	    )
241	
242	
243	def verify_records(
244	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
245	    *,
246	    secret: bytes | None = None,
247	) -> None:
248	    """Verify a sequence of entries forms an intact chain (order, linkage, content).
249	
250	    Pass the same ``secret`` the trail was keyed with; an unkeyed trail verifies with
251	    ``secret=None``. A keyed trail will not verify under the wrong/absent secret.
252	    """
253	    prev = _GENESIS
254	    for i, e in enumerate(entries):
255	        if e.seq != i:
256	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} has seq {e.seq} (reordered or removed)")
257	        if e.prev_hash != prev:
258	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} prev_hash breaks the chain")
259	        recomputed = _digest(e.seq, e.kind, e.summary, e.payload, e.timestamp, e.prev_hash, secret)
260	        if not hmac.compare_digest(recomputed, e.entry_hash):
261	            raise ProvenanceError(
262	                f"provenance entry {i} content was altered, or the wrong key was used (hash mismatch)"
263	            )
264	        prev = e.entry_hash
265	
266	
267	def from_records(
268	    records: "list[Mapping[str, Any]] | tuple[Mapping[str, Any], ...]",
269	    *,
270	    secret: bytes | None = None,
271	) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
272	    """Rebuild entries from :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` output and verify the chain."""
273	    _required = ("seq", "kind", "summary", "payload", "timestamp", "prev_hash", "entry_hash")
274	    rebuilt: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
275	    for i, r in enumerate(records):
276	        missing = [k for k in _required if k not in r]
277	        if missing:
278	            # A malformed/incompatible trail record must surface as the module's typed error, not a
279	            # raw KeyError, so a caller catching ProvenanceError to handle a corrupt trail file
280	            # covers this case too (pass 4, #G-012).
281	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance record {i} is missing field(s) {missing}")
282	        rebuilt.append(
283	            ProvenanceEntry(
284	                seq=r["seq"],
285	                kind=r["kind"],
286	                summary=r["summary"],
287	                payload=r["payload"],
288	                timestamp=r["timestamp"],
289	                prev_hash=r["prev_hash"],
290	                entry_hash=r["entry_hash"],
291	            )
292	        )
293	    entries = tuple(rebuilt)
294	    verify_records(entries, secret=secret)
295	    return entries
296

1	"""Study runner — the composed, governed, end-to-end pipeline (ADR-0010).
2	
3	The phase machine (:mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.phases`) *names* the ordered stages a
4	study moves through; the firewall runners enforce the methodology; the seams provide tracing,
5	experiment tracking, data versioning, and (in the adapter's callables) reasoning. This module
6	composes them into a single runnable flow, engine-owned so the ordering and the governance
7	handoffs are guaranteed once, not re-implemented per study:
8	
9	    INGEST → BASELINE(blind, Firewall A) → [DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B)]
10	           → [ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A, shared baseline)] → REPORT
11	
12	At every step it (a) records an append-only provenance entry *before the next step runs*
13	(GOVERNANCE §3), (b) opens a trace span (observability seam), optionally (c) versions the source
14	and logs to an experiment tracker, and (d) enforces the methodological invariants structurally:
15	the baseline is built blind, the split ids are validated against the corpus, hypotheses are
16	verifiably pre-registered before confirmation, a human gate reviews before *every* confirmatory
17	step, claim-derived hypotheses are locked before the baseline exists, and the visited phase
18	sequence must equal the mode's required sequence.
19	
20	A study supplies the *domain* via a :class:`StudyPlan` (parser, baseline builder, the
21	discover/confirm/hypothesis_for callables, the claims source, optional feature builders); the
22	engine owns the *order, the firewalls, the gate, the provenance, the tracing, and the
23	versioning*. Reasoning enters only inside the study's own callables (which may use
24	:mod:`assay_engine.reasoning`); any such LLM output is *judgment*, so it should be wrapped as an
25	:class:`~assay_engine.methodology.fence.Interpretation` and never fed back into a measurement
26	(the fence). The runner imports no adapter (ARCHITECTURE §3).
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import datetime as _dt
32	import logging
33	import time
34	import uuid
35	import warnings
36	from contextlib import ExitStack
37	from dataclasses import dataclass
38	from pathlib import Path
39	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable, Mapping, cast
40	
41	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping, unfreeze
42	from assay_engine.baseline.determinism import corpus_fingerprint
43	from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact, BaselineBuilder
44	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ClaimRecord, claim_set_fingerprint
45	from assay_engine.contracts.features import FeatureMatrix
46	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
47	from assay_engine.contracts.study import StudyDefinition, StudyMode
48	from assay_engine.methodology.adjudication import (
49	    ClaimConfirmer,
50	    SourceScorecard,
51	    adjudicate_with_baseline,
52	)
53	from assay_engine.methodology.discovery import HeldOutConfirmer, subset_corpus
54	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import (
55	    ClaimBlindGuard,
56	    DiscoverConfirmSplit,
57	    FirewallViolation,
58	)
59	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisOrigin
60	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import TimestampAuthority, require_preregistered
61	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict
62	from assay_engine.observability.tracing import (
63	    OtelTracer,
64	    Tracer,
65	    bootstrap_tracing,
66	    run_trace_context,
67	)
68	from assay_engine.observability.tracking import ExperimentTracker
69	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import GateDecision, GateError
70	from assay_engine.orchestration.phases import Phase, required_phases
71	from assay_engine.persistence.versioning import DataVersioner
72	from assay_engine.provenance import Clock, ProvenanceEntry, ProvenanceTrail
73	
74	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
75	
76	_log = logging.getLogger("assay_engine.pipeline")
77	
78	DiscoverFn = Callable[[Corpus], Iterable[Hypothesis]]
79	
80	
81	class IngestionError(ValueError):
82	    """Ingestion failed (the parser raised or produced an unusable corpus)."""
83	
84	
85	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
86	class GateReview:
87	    """The packet handed to a gate handler at a governance transition.
88	
89	    ``payload`` is a deep-frozen :class:`~assay_engine._frozen.FrozenDict` (immutable +
90	    hashable, #113). Because that is a ``Mapping`` and not a ``dict`` subclass, ``json.dumps``
91	    cannot serialize it directly — an operator-review handler that logs/serializes the payload
92	    must call :meth:`payload_dict` first (#141).
93	    """
94	
95	    gate: str
96	    frm: Phase
97	    to: Phase
98	    summary: str
99	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
100	
101	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
102	        # deep-freeze the payload so this frozen record is truly immutable + hashable (#113)
103	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
104	
105	    def payload_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
106	        """Return the payload as a plain, deeply-thawed, JSON-serializable ``dict`` (#141)."""
107	        return cast("dict[str, Any]", unfreeze(self.payload))
108	
109	
110	GateHandler = Callable[[GateReview], GateDecision]
111	
112	
113	def _engine_claim_fingerprint(claim_records: list[ClaimRecord]) -> str:
114	    """Engine-computed content hash of the EXACT claim set that will be scored (#137, #F-001).
115	
116	    Delegates to the public :func:`~assay_engine.contracts.claims.claim_set_fingerprint` so the
117	    engine's authoritative fingerprint and the scheme adapters must implement in
118	    ``claim_fingerprint()`` are one and the same canonical function — the engine recomputes it
119	    over the materialized records (in scored order) and enforces agreement with the source's
120	    self-report, so an auditor re-deriving the fingerprint from the scored claims gets the value
121	    the trail records.
122	    """
123	    return claim_set_fingerprint(claim_records)
124	
125	
126	def _require_unique_ids(ids: list[str], what: str) -> None:
127	    """Raise FirewallViolation if ``ids`` contains a duplicate (#138).
128	
129	    Identity (claim↔hypothesis↔verdict) must be unique within a run: a repeated id would
130	    otherwise be counted once per occurrence, inflating the scorecard denominator and biasing
131	    alignment_rate / verdict accounting.
132	    """
133	    seen: set[str] = set()
134	    dupes: list[str] = []
135	    for i in ids:
136	        if i in seen:
137	            dupes.append(i)
138	        seen.add(i)
139	    if dupes:
140	        raise FirewallViolation(
141	            f"duplicate {what} within one run: {sorted(set(dupes))[:3]} — identity must be "
142	            "unique (a repeat would inflate the scorecard denominator / verdict accounting)"
143	        )

146	def auto_approve(review: GateReview) -> GateDecision:
147	    """A non-interactive gate handler: approve and record the decision.
148	
149	    This is the explicit "no operator" choice — it is **not** the default, so a study cannot
150	    silently bypass human review by omitting a handler (it must consciously pass this). Replace
151	    with an operator-driven handler (e.g. one wired to the LangGraph gate interrupt/resume in
152	    :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration`) for genuine human-in-the-loop parking; the handler may
153	    return ``approved=False`` to block the transition.
154	    """
155	    return GateDecision(
156	        approved=True,
157	        gate=review.gate,
158	        reason="non-interactive auto-approval",
159	        evidence={"transition": f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}"},
160	    )

169	    against the declared modes: discovery needs ``split``/``discover``/``confirm_held_out``;
170	    adjudication needs ``hypothesis_for``/``confirm_claim`` (and a ``claims_source`` on the
171	    definition).

179	    discover: DiscoverFn | None = None

185	        modes = self.definition.modes

188	                n
189	                for n, v in (
190	                    ("split", self.split),
191	                    ("discover", self.discover),
192	                    ("confirm_held_out", self.confirm_held_out),
193	                )

199	            missing = [
200	                n
201	                for n, v in (
202	                    ("hypothesis_for", self.hypothesis_for),
203	                    ("confirm_claim", self.confirm_claim),
204	                )
205	                if v is None
206	            ]
207	            if missing:

212	class StudyResult:
213	    """The reproducibility package: the artifacts + the append-only trail that produced them."""

215	    study: str

226	    def persist_trail(self, path: str | Path) -> Path:
227	        """Write the hash-chained provenance trail to ``path`` as JSON and return it (#F-045).
228	
229	        ``run_study`` keeps the trail only in memory (``self.provenance``); a caller that needs a
230	        durable, re-verifiable audit trail across process exit must persist it. The written
231	        records round-trip through :func:`~assay_engine.provenance.from_records` and re-check via
232	        :func:`~assay_engine.provenance.verify_records`. Call ``tracker.log_artifact`` on the
233	        returned path to attach it to the experiment run.
234	        """
235	        import json
236	
237	        from assay_engine.provenance import entries_to_records
238	
239	        out = Path(path)
240	        out.write_text(
241	            json.dumps(list(entries_to_records(list(self.provenance))), indent=2),
242	            encoding="utf-8",
243	        )
244	        return out

262	def run_study(
263	    plan: StudyPlan,
264	    *,
265	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
266	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
267	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
268	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
269	    clock: Clock | None = None,
270	    secret: bytes | None = None,
271	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
272	    verify_trail: bool = True,
273	) -> StudyResult:
274	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
275	
276	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
277	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
278	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
279	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
280	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
281	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
282	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
283	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
284	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
285	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
286	
287	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
288	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
289	    """
290	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
291	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
292	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
293	    if secret is None and trail is None:
294	        # An engine-created unkeyed trail is tamper-evident (a naive edit breaks the SHA-256
295	        # chain) but NOT forgery-resistant: an adversary with write access can edit an entry and
296	        # recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain. That is fine for local reproducibility but
297	        # insufficient for an audit trail that crosses a trust boundary. Flag the insecure
298	        # default rather than letting it pass silently (pass 3, #F-049); pass a `secret` to key
299	        # the trail with HMAC, or `clock=`-driven deterministic repro if forgery is out of scope.
300	        warnings.warn(
301	            "run_study provenance trail is UNKEYED (secret=None) — tamper-evident but not "
302	            "forgery-resistant. Pass secret=<bytes> to HMAC-key the trail for audit trails that "
303	            "cross a trust boundary.",
304	            stacklevel=2,
305	        )
306	    defn = plan.definition
307	    modes = defn.modes
308	    required = required_phases(modes)
309	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
310	    visited: list[Phase] = []
311	    run_id: str | None = None
312	
313	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
314	        visited.append(phase)
315	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
316	
317	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
318	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
319	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
320	            return
321	        try:
322	            fn(tracker, run_id)
323	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
324	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
325	
326	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
327	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
328	        decision = gate_handler(review)
329	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
330	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
331	        trail.record(
332	            "gate",
333	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
334	            gate=review.gate,
335	            approved=approved,
336	            reason=reason,
337	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
338	        )
339	        if not approved:
340	            raise GateError(
341	                f"gate {review.gate!r} blocked {review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}: {reason}"
342	            )
343	
344	    trail.record(
345	        "run_start",
346	        f"study {defn.name!r} starting",
347	        study=defn.name,
348	        modes=sorted(m.value for m in modes),
349	        research_questions=list(defn.research_questions),
350	    )
351	    if tracker is not None:
352	        try:
353	            run_id = tracker.start_run(
354	                defn.name,
355	                {
356	                    "modes": ",".join(sorted(m.value for m in modes)),
357	                    "n_research_questions": len(defn.research_questions),
358	                },
359	            )
360	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
361	            # run_id stays None → every track() call silently no-ops and ALL metrics are dropped
362	            # for the whole run. Record it to the trail AND emit a structured warning so an
363	            # operator seeing no MLflow run knows why instead of guessing (pass 3, #F-028).
364	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
365	            _log.warning(
366	                "experiment tracker start_run failed (%s: %s); run metrics will not be logged "
367	                "to the tracker for this study",
368	                type(exc).__name__,
369	                exc,
370	            )
371	
372	    ok = False
373	    started_at = time.monotonic()
374	    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
375	    try:
376	        # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
377	        # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122).
378	        # Done INSIDE the try so a tracing-setup failure is still covered by the finally's
379	        # tracker.end_run cleanup — an already-started tracker run must never leak in RUNNING
380	        # state (the #110 class of bug) if bootstrap/context entry raises (#156). Best-effort.
381	        bootstrap_tracing()
382	        corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex
383	        trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id))
384	        # ---- INGEST ----
385	        with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):
386	            enter(Phase.INGEST)
387	            try:
388	                corpus = defn.parser.parse(plan.source)
389	                source_fp = defn.parser.source_fingerprint(plan.source)
390	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize adapter/IO ingestion failures
391	                raise IngestionError(f"parser failed to ingest {plan.source!r}: {exc}") from exc
392	            # The parser Protocol return type is unenforced at runtime; a misimplemented adapter
393	            # that returns None/list/dict would otherwise slip past the normalizer above and die
394	            # at `corpus.units` with an opaque AttributeError, violating the documented
395	            # "Raises IngestionError on a bad source" contract (#134).
396	            if not isinstance(corpus, Corpus):
397	                raise IngestionError(f"parser returned {type(corpus).__name__}, expected Corpus")
398	            if not isinstance(source_fp, str):
399	                raise IngestionError(
400	                    f"parser.source_fingerprint returned {type(source_fp).__name__}, expected str"
401	                )
402	            if not corpus.units:
403	                raise IngestionError("ingestion produced an empty corpus")
404	            cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
405	            source_version: str | None = None
406	            if versioner is not None:
407	                # The versioner is an optional seam; a storage failure (PermissionError,
408	                # OSError) must surface as the documented IngestionError, not a raw untyped
409	                # exception that the caller cannot distinguish from a fatal firewall breach
410	                # (pass 3, #F-008). Versioning is part of ingesting the source, so a failure to
411	                # content-address it is an ingestion failure.
412	                try:
413	                    source_version = versioner.put(str(plan.source))
414	                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize optional-seam storage failures
415	                    raise IngestionError(
416	                        f"data versioner failed to store {plan.source!r}: {exc}"
417	                    ) from exc
418	            trail.record(
419	                "ingest",
420	                "parsed source into canonical corpus",
421	                source_fingerprint=source_fp,
422	                corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
423	                n_units=len(corpus.units),
424	                n_relations=len(corpus.relations),
425	                source_version=source_version,
426	            )
427	            # SLO-relevant scale metrics to the tracker so corpus-size↔latency regressions are
428	            # analysable from the experiment store, not only the provenance trail (pass 3,
429	            # #F-029). Best-effort via track() (no-ops if no tracker/run_id).
430	            track(_count_logger("n_units", float(len(corpus.units))))
431	            track(_count_logger("n_relations", float(len(corpus.relations))))
432	            # Compute the corpus unit-id set ONCE and reuse it for both the split-id and feature-id
433	            # validations (pass 4, #G-023 — was built twice on the same corpus).
434	            corpus_unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
435	            # split ids must reference real corpus units (no silent partition drift, audit H4)
436	            if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
437	                assert plan.split is not None
438	                unknown = (
439	                    set(plan.split.discovery_ids) | set(plan.split.confirm_ids)
440	                ) - corpus_unit_ids
441	                if unknown:
442	                    raise FirewallViolation(
443	                        f"split references {len(unknown)} id(s) absent from the corpus "
444	                        f"(e.g. {sorted(unknown)[:3]}) — partition would silently drop units"
445	                    )
446	            # optional dataset features (recorded + returned; the baseline builder is blind-built
447	            # from the corpus, so features are provenance/output, not a baseline input here)
448	            feature_matrices: list[FeatureMatrix] = []
449	            for fb in defn.feature_builders:
450	                fm = fb.build(corpus)
451	                # features must describe THIS corpus's units — no fictional or duplicate ids
452	                # (the feature-side analogue of the split-drift guard above).
453	                if len(set(fm.unit_ids)) != len(fm.unit_ids):
454	                    raise FirewallViolation(
455	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned duplicate unit_ids"
456	                    )
457	                ghost = set(fm.unit_ids) - corpus_unit_ids
458	                if ghost:
459	                    raise FirewallViolation(
460	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned {len(ghost)} unit_id(s) "
461	                        f"absent from the corpus (e.g. {sorted(ghost)[:3]})"
462	                    )
463	                feature_matrices.append(fm)
464	                trail.record(
465	                    "features",
466	                    f"built feature matrix ({len(fm.feature_names)} features)",
467	                    provides=list(fb.provides),
468	                    n_units=len(fm.unit_ids),
469	                    feature_names=list(fm.feature_names),
470	                )
471	
472	        # ---- BASELINE (blind — Firewall A). Capture the ordering instant BEFORE the build. ----
473	        baseline_instant = _now()
474	        with tracer.span("phase:BASELINE"):
475	            enter(Phase.BASELINE)
476	            guard: ClaimBlindGuard[Any] = ClaimBlindGuard()
477	            with guard.sealed():
478	                baseline = plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
479	            if baseline.corpus_fingerprint != cfp:
480	                raise FirewallViolation(
481	                    "baseline corpus_fingerprint does not match the ingested corpus — the baseline "
482	                    "was not built from this corpus"
483	                )
484	            trail.record(
485	                "baseline",
486	                "built blind baseline (Firewall A)",
487	                corpus_fingerprint=baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
488	                contents=sorted(baseline.contents),
489	                determinism=dict(baseline.determinism),
490	            )
491	
492	        discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...] = ()
493	        scorecard: SourceScorecard | None = None
494	
495	        # ---- DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B) ----
496	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
497	            assert plan.split is not None and plan.discover is not None
498	            assert plan.confirm_held_out is not None
499	            discovery_corpus = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.discovery_ids)
500	            held_out = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.confirm_ids)
501	
502	            with tracer.span("phase:DISCOVERY"):
503	                enter(Phase.DISCOVERY)
504	                if not discovery_corpus.units:
505	                    raise FirewallViolation("discovery partition selects no corpus units")
506	                hypotheses = list(plan.discover(discovery_corpus))  # only the discovery partition
507	                if not hypotheses:
508	                    # discover() surfaced nothing — a vacuous run indistinguishable from a broken
509	                    # callable that forgot to return. Fail loud (pass 3, #F-042).
510	                    raise FirewallViolation(
511	                        "discover() returned no hypotheses — a discovery run with zero "
512	                        "hypotheses is vacuous and cannot produce a confirmatory verdict"
513	                    )
514	                # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated hypothesis_id would be counted once
515	                # per occurrence in the verdict accounting (#138)
516	                _require_unique_ids([h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses], "hypothesis_id")
517	                trail.record(
518	                    "discovery",
519	                    f"data surfaced {len(hypotheses)} candidate hypotheses",
520	                    n=len(hypotheses),
521	                    ids=[h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
522	                )
523	
524	            with tracer.span("phase:PREREGISTER"):
525	                enter(Phase.PREREGISTER)
526	                if not held_out.units:
527	                    raise FirewallViolation("confirmation partition selects no corpus units")
528	                preregister_instant = _now()
529	                for h in hypotheses:
530	                    if h.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY:
531	                        raise FirewallViolation(
532	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} is {h.origin.value!r}; the discovery "
533	                            "phase expects DISCOVERY-origin (data-surfaced) hypotheses"
534	                        )
535	                    vt = require_preregistered(
536	                        h, authority=plan.authority, not_after=preregister_instant
537	                    )
538	                    trail.record(
539	                        "preregister",
540	                        f"locked hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
541	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
542	                        digest=vt.digest,
543	                        locked_at=vt.instant.isoformat(),
544	                    )
545	                run_gate(
546	                    GateReview(
547	                        gate="review-locked-hypotheses",
548	                        frm=Phase.PREREGISTER,
549	                        to=Phase.CONFIRM,
550	                        summary="review locked hypotheses before confirmatory testing",
551	                        payload={
552	                            "hypothesis_ids": [h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
553	                            "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
554	                        },
555	                    )
556	                )
557	
558	            with tracer.span("phase:CONFIRM"):
559	                enter(Phase.CONFIRM)
560	                confirmed: list[Verdict] = []
561	                for h in hypotheses:
562	                    verdict = plan.confirm_held_out(h, held_out)  # only the held-out partition
563	                    # A confirmer that forgets to `return` yields None; accessing
564	                    # verdict.hypothesis_id would raise an opaque AttributeError instead of the
565	                    # documented typed error (pass 3, #F-021). Guard the contract explicitly.
566	                    if not isinstance(verdict, Verdict):
567	                        raise FirewallViolation(
568	                            f"confirm_held_out returned {type(verdict).__name__} for hypothesis "
569	                            f"{h.hypothesis_id!r} — expected a Verdict"
570	                        )
571	                    if verdict.hypothesis_id != h.hypothesis_id:
572	                        raise FirewallViolation(
573	                            f"verdict reports hypothesis_id {verdict.hypothesis_id!r} for "
574	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
575	                        )
576	                    confirmed.append(verdict)
577	                    trail.record(
578	                        "verdict",
579	                        f"confirmed {h.hypothesis_id!r}: {verdict.label.value}",
580	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
581	                        label=verdict.label.value,
582	                        rule=verdict.decision_rule,
583	                    )
584	                discovery_verdicts = tuple(confirmed)
585	                for lbl in ("supported", "contradicted", "indeterminate"):
586	                    n = sum(1 for v in discovery_verdicts if v.label.value == lbl)
587	                    track(_count_logger(f"discovery_{lbl}", float(n)))
588	
589	        # ---- ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A; the baseline built above is reused, blind) ----
590	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
591	            assert defn.claims_source is not None
592	            assert plan.hypothesis_for is not None and plan.confirm_claim is not None
593	            claim_records = list(defn.claims_source.claims())
594	            if not claim_records:
595	                raise ValueError(
596	                    "adjudication mode but the claims source yielded no claims — nothing to adjudicate"
597	                )
598	            # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated claim_id would inflate the scorecard (#138)
599	            _require_unique_ids([c.claim_id for c in claim_records], "claim_id")
600	            track(_count_logger("n_claims", float(len(claim_records))))  # SLO metric (#F-029)
601	            # The engine fingerprints the EXACT claims it will score (authoritative), and records
602	            # the source's self-report separately for cross-check rather than trusting it (#137).
603	            engine_claim_fp = _engine_claim_fingerprint(claim_records)
604	            source_claim_fp = defn.claims_source.claim_fingerprint()
605	            # Enforce — do not merely *record* — the fingerprint agreement (pass 3, #F-001).
606	            # Pass-2 #137 made the engine fingerprint authoritative and surfaced the match as a
607	            # boolean in the gate payload, but a non-interactive gate (auto_approve) approves
608	            # regardless, so a source whose claim_fingerprint() lies about a claim set that
609	            # claims() does not actually yield completed a run with the discrepancy silently
610	            # recorded. The source's self-report is a content commitment for pre-registration;
611	            # a disagreement means the scored claims are not the set the source attested to —
612	            # a provenance-integrity breach the engine must refuse, not defer to a human.
613	            if source_claim_fp != engine_claim_fp:
614	                raise FirewallViolation(
615	                    "claims source self-reported claim_fingerprint "
616	                    f"{source_claim_fp!r} but the engine computed {engine_claim_fp!r} over the "
617	                    "claims actually yielded — the scored claim set does not match the source's "
618	                    "attested fingerprint (provenance-integrity breach)"
619	                )
620	            prev_phase = Phase.CONFIRM if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes else Phase.BASELINE
621	            run_gate(
622	                GateReview(
623	                    gate="review-baseline-and-claims",
624	                    frm=prev_phase,
625	                    to=Phase.ADJUDICATE,
626	                    summary="review the blind baseline and the external claim set before adjudication",
627	                    payload={
628	                        "n_claims": len(claim_records),
629	                        "claim_ids": [c.claim_id for c in claim_records],
630	                        "claim_fingerprint": engine_claim_fp,  # engine-computed over scored claims
631	                        "source_reported_claim_fingerprint": source_claim_fp,  # unverified self-report
632	                        "claim_fingerprint_matches_source": engine_claim_fp == source_claim_fp,
633	                        "baseline_fingerprint": baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
634	                        "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
635	                    },
636	                )
637	            )
638	            with tracer.span("phase:ADJUDICATE"):
639	                enter(Phase.ADJUDICATE)
640	
641	                def _record_adjudication_step(h: Hypothesis, v: Verdict) -> None:
642	                    trail.record(
643	                        "preregister",
644	                        f"locked claim hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
645	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
646	                        source_claim_id=h.source_claim_id,
647	                        locked_at=h.locked_at,
648	                    )
649	                    trail.record(
650	                        "verdict",
651	                        f"adjudicated {v.hypothesis_id!r}: {v.label.value}",
652	                        hypothesis_id=v.hypothesis_id,
653	                        label=v.label.value,
654	                        rule=v.decision_rule,
655	                    )
656	
657	                # not_after = baseline_instant: a claim-derived hypothesis must have been locked
658	                # before the (shared) baseline existed. The SAME materialized claim_records that
659	                # were gated/recorded are scored (#95).
660	                scorecard = adjudicate_with_baseline(
661	                    baseline,
662	                    claim_records,
663	                    hypothesis_for=plan.hypothesis_for,
664	                    confirm=plan.confirm_claim,
665	                    authority=plan.authority,
666	                    not_after=baseline_instant,
667	                    source_name=defn.name,
668	                    on_step=_record_adjudication_step,
669	                )
670	            with tracer.span("phase:SCORE"):
671	                enter(Phase.SCORE)
672	                trail.record(
673	                    "score",
674	                    f"source scorecard for {defn.name!r}",
675	                    n_supported=scorecard.n_supported,
676	                    n_contradicted=scorecard.n_contradicted,
677	                    n_indeterminate=scorecard.n_indeterminate,
678	                    alignment_rate=scorecard.alignment_rate,
679	                )
680	                sc = scorecard
681	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_supported", float(sc.n_supported)))
682	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_contradicted", float(sc.n_contradicted)))
683	                track(
684	                    lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_indeterminate", float(sc.n_indeterminate))
685	                )
686	                ar = sc.alignment_rate
687	                if ar is not None:
688	                    track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "alignment_rate", float(ar)))
689	
690	        # ---- REPORT ----
691	        with tracer.span("phase:REPORT"):
692	            enter(Phase.REPORT)
693	            trail.record(
694	                "report",
695	                "assembled reproducibility package",
696	                n_discovery_verdicts=len(discovery_verdicts),
697	                has_scorecard=scorecard is not None,
698	            )
699	
700	        if tuple(visited) != required:
701	            raise FirewallViolation(
702	                f"visited phase sequence {[p.name for p in visited]} != required "
703	                f"{[p.name for p in required]}"
704	            )
705	        if verify_trail:
706	            # Re-verify the whole chain before handing back the result. This re-hashes every
707	            # entry — O(N) — which is redundant for an in-memory trail this call built and never
708	            # mutated (each entry was hashed correctly when appended). It is a cheap safety net in
709	            # the common case but a real cost for very large trails, so a perf-sensitive caller
710	            # that trusts the in-process trail may pass verify_trail=False (pass 3, #F-036). The
711	            # important re-verification — of a trail deserialized from an external store — is the
712	            # caller's verify_records() on from_records(), which this flag does not affect.
713	            trail.verify()  # the provenance chain must be intact before we hand back the result
714	        # Terminal success entry (pass 3, #F-006): a phase 'report' record is not a run-level
715	        # terminus, so a trail truncated mid-run is otherwise indistinguishable from one that
716	        # completed. Record run_end ONLY after verify() passes, so its presence as the last
717	        # entry is an affirmative "this run finished with an intact chain" marker. The payload is
718	        # deterministic (no wall-clock) so the provenance-hash determinism guarantee holds — the
719	        # non-deterministic run duration goes to the experiment tracker as an SLO metric instead.
720	        trail.record(
721	            "run_end",
722	            f"study {defn.name!r} completed successfully",
723	            study=defn.name,
724	            experiment_run_id=run_id,
725	            n_provenance_entries=len(trail.entries) + 1,  # +1 counts this terminal entry itself
726	        )
727	        track(_count_logger("run_duration_s", time.monotonic() - started_at))  # SLO metric (#F-029)
728	        # Correlate the hash-chained trail to the experiment run (#G-009): persist it as a JSON
729	        # artifact on the tracker so an auditor opening the run can retrieve the provenance that
730	        # produced its metrics. Best-effort, like the other tracker calls — never aborts the run.
731	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
732	
733	            def _log_trail(t: ExperimentTracker, rid: str) -> None:
734	                import json
735	                import os
736	                import tempfile
737	
738	                from assay_engine.provenance import entries_to_records
739	
740	                with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
741	                    "w", suffix="_provenance.json", delete=False, encoding="utf-8"
742	                ) as fh:
743	                    json.dump(list(entries_to_records(list(trail.entries))), fh, indent=2)
744	                    path = fh.name
745	                # Clean up the scratch file after the tracker has copied it into its store, so a
746	                # tracked run does not leak a temp file (with provenance contents) on every call
747	                # (#H-013). The tracker reads/copies synchronously in log_artifact.
748	                try:
749	                    t.log_artifact(rid, path)
750	                finally:
751	                    try:
752	                        os.unlink(path)
753	                    except OSError:
754	                        pass
755	
756	            track(_log_trail)
757	        ok = True
758	    except Exception as exc:
759	        # Record a terminal failure entry BEFORE re-raising so the abort reason is recoverable
760	        # from the (caller-owned) trail, not lost (#109).
761	        last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start"
762	        try:
763	            trail.record(
764	                "run_failed",
765	                f"{type(exc).__name__} during {last_phase}",
766	                error_type=type(exc).__name__,
767	                message=str(exc)[:2000],
768	                last_phase=last_phase,
769	            )
770	        except Exception as inner:  # noqa: BLE001 — never mask the original failure
771	            # The run_failed entry could not be recorded (payload unserializable, clock raised,
772	            # trail lock contended). Don't silently swallow — emit a structured warning so the
773	            # failure reason is at least in the logs, even though it is absent from the trail
774	            # (pass 3, #F-030). The original exception is still re-raised below.
775	            _log.warning(
776	                "could not record run_failed provenance entry (%s: %s)",
777	                type(inner).__name__,
778	                inner,
779	            )
780	        raise
781	    finally:
782	        trace_ctx.close()
783	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
784	            try:
785	                # a failed run must be distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110)
786	                tracker.end_run(run_id, status="FINISHED" if ok else "FAILED")
787	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — best-effort cleanup
788	                pass
789	
790	    return StudyResult(
791	        study=defn.name,
792	        corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
793	        baseline=baseline,
794	        discovery_verdicts=discovery_verdicts,
795	        scorecard=scorecard,
796	        phases=tuple(visited),
797	        provenance=trail.entries,
798	        source_version=source_version,
799	        feature_matrices=tuple(feature_matrices),
800	        experiment_run_id=run_id,
801	    )
802
